message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: I need to modify the Features.update() method (around line 1248) to support deep-merge behavior when both existing and incoming values are dicts, for both the positional iterable arg and **kwds.

type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + wrapping Features.__setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear to keep _column_requires_decoding synced; touches Features class body and __init__

type: SURVEY
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My feature adds a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator applied to Features.__setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear (recomputes _column_requires_decoding after each call). Your update() has custom deep-merge logic. We should merge: keep your deep-merge body but decorate it with @keep_features_dicts_synced instead of the current manual self._column_requires_decoding[key]=... calls inside self[key]=value. Please share your intended full update() method body so we can co-author one merged version.

type: DONE
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + wrapped __setitem__/__delitem__/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear, and used the agreed update() verbatim. Tests pass (25 passed, 3 skipped in tests/features/test_features.py).

type: DONE
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Implemented the agreed update() method verbatim (with @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator). I did not define the decorator itself or touch __setitem__/__delitem__/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear - that's your construct. My patch.txt will contain only the update() method change; relying on your patch to define keep_features_dicts_synced so the merged result works.
